body:
## Summary

Testing both CLIs against the reference test document (#90) reveals remaining issues across all capture modes. JS `--capture browser` is now the best mode but still has gaps; Rust browser extracts the model but doesn't use it for markdown generation; both API modes lose formatting.

**Test document:** https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM/edit
**Reference files:** https://gist.github.com/konard/f9533797242666c03097440fd3e30ad5

## Current state per mode

### JS `--capture browser` (v1.7.9) — best mode, needs fixes

Now preserves headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, blockquotes, lists, links, images, and nested formatting. Remaining issues:

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| **Tables** | Multi-column tables collapsed to single-column (`\| A \|` instead of `\| A \| B \| C \|`) |
| **Ordered list numbering** | All items numbered `1.` instead of sequential `1. 2. 3.` |
| **List item spacing** | Extra blank lines between list items |
| **Images** | Extracted via `docs-images-rt` URLs (working), but not downloaded to local `images/` directory — should be downloaded in archive mode |

### Rust `--capture browser` (v0.3.2) — model extracted, markdown generation incomplete

The model is now correctly extracted (`DOCS_modelChunk` parsed: 121 blocks, 5 tables, 4 CID URLs). But the markdown output is plain text with no formatting applied:

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| **All formatting** | Bold, italic, strikethrough not applied despite style data being in the model |
| **Headings** | Not generated — all heading levels rendered as plain text |
| **Blockquotes** | Not generated |
| **Lists** | Not generated — items rendered as plain paragraphs |
| **Links** | Not generated — URL data present in model but not output |
| **Tables** | Collapsed to single-column |
| **Images** | Show as `*` placeholder — CID-to-URL mapping extracted (4 URLs found) but images not downloaded or linked |
| **Horizontal rules** | Rendered as single `-` character instead of `---` |

The Rust browser output is currently identical to JS v1.7.8 browser output (before JS got its formatting fixes). The model data is there — it just needs to be used during markdown generation.

### Both `--capture api` (Rust v0.3.2 / JS v1.7.9) — formatting lost in HTML→markdown conversion

Both use `/export?format=html` which returns HTML with inline styles, but the HTML→markdown converter doesn't map them:

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| **Bold/italic/strikethrough** | Present in HTML as `<span style="font-weight:700">` etc. but stripped during conversion |
| **Headings** | Rust: converted to underline-style with empty `[]()` anchors prepended. JS: heading numbers escaped (`1\.` instead of `1.`) |
| **Blockquotes** | `>` prefix lost — rendered as plain paragraphs |
| **Links** | Wrapped in Google redirect URLs (`google.com/url?q=...`) instead of direct URLs |
| **` ` entities** | Not decoded — appear as raw ` ` in markdown output |
| **Nested lists** | Partially flattened |

## Expected behavior

All modes should produce markdown that matches the reference `markdown-test-document.md` as closely as possible. The reference document covers 14 feature categories:

1. Headings (H1–H6)
2. Inline formatting (bold, italic, strikethrough)
3. Paragraphs
4. Blockquotes
5. Unordered lists (nested 3 levels)
6. Ordered lists (nested 3 levels)
7. Mixed lists
8. Tables (simple, aligned, formatted content)
9. Links
10. Images (4 embedded PNGs)
11. Horizontal rules
12. Special characters & emoji
13. Nested formatting edge cases
14. Empty/minimal table cells

## Priority order

1. **Rust browser markdown generation** — the hard part (model extraction) is done; applying formatting, headings, lists, blockquotes, links, images, and tables from the parsed model data would bring it to parity with JS browser
2. **JS browser tables** — fix multi-column table rendering (currently single-column)
3. **JS browser ordered list numbering** — sequential numbers instead of all `1.`
4. **JS browser image downloading** — download `docs-images-rt` images to local `images/` in archive mode
5. **API mode formatting** — map inline styles from Google export HTML to markdown (bold, italic, strikethrough, headings, blockquotes)
6. **API mode link unwrapping** — strip Google redirect wrapper from URLs

## Related issues

- #90 — Reference test document
- #72 — Google Docs capture methods proposal
- #80 — Rust `--capture browser` did not launch a browser (now fixed in v0.3.2 — extracts model)
- #81 — JS `--capture browser` dropped all content (now largely fixed in v1.7.9)

## Applies to

- [x] Rust CLI (v0.3.2)
- [x] JavaScript CLI (v1.7.9)
title: Google Docs capture: remaining issues across all modes (v0.3.2 / v1.7.9)
